title: valeriana locusta subsp. olitoria
intro: Valeriana locusta subsp. olitoria, also known as Wild Valerian or Stinking Valerian, is a subspecies of the Valeriana locusta plant that belongs to the Valerianaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic and sedative effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, as well as to alleviate symptoms of menopause and menstrual cramps. Wild Valerian root is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is also dried and used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
